title: ranunculus flabellaris f. rosiflorus
intro: Ranunculus flabellaris f. rosiflorus, also known as the buttercup, is a flowering plant species in the genus Ranunculus. It is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers with five petals and a cluster of small, white or pinkish stamens. The plant is known to possess medicinal properties, particularly its root, which contains ranunculin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Ranunculus flabellaris f. rosiflorus has been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is often made into teas, tinctures, and salves for its medicinal benefits.
